model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Vanderbilt holds a decisive edge in offensive efficiency, ranking 6th in KenPom adjusted offense and 10th overall in Torvik, which should exploit Nebraska's weaker defense despite the Cornhuskers' top-10 defensive rating. While Nebraska's defense is elite, their offense is only 55th-62nd in efficiency, and they lack a consistent go-to scorer, which could limit their ability to keep pace with Vanderbilt's high-powered attack. Vanderbilt's recent form in the SEC and superior overall efficiency metrics make them the more reliable pick, though Nebraska's defensive prowess gives them a credible path if they can force turnovers and control tempo.

reasoningStep.title: Investigate team ratings and matchup dynamics
reasoningStep.summary: Vanderbilt ranks 12th in KenPom and 10th in Torvik with elite offensive efficiency (6th in KenPom adjO), while Nebraska ranks 14th in KenPom and 19th in Torvik with a top-7 defense but a 55th-62nd ranked offense. The matchup favors Vanderbilt's offense against Nebraska's defense, but Nebraska's defensive strength could keep it close.
reasoningStep.evidence: Vanderbilt: KenPom rank 12, adjO 127.3 (6th), adjD 99.2; Torvik rank 10, adjOE 127.8 (6th), adjDE 99.2 (28th). Nebraska: KenPom rank 14, adjO 118.6 (55th), adjD 92.5 (7th); Torvik rank 19, adjOE 117.3 (62nd), adjDE 93.5 (7th).

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: March Madness 2026: Bracket, Top Teams & Tournament Preview Store Home Blog Scoreboard LOG IN Newsletter NCAA March Madness 2026 basketball bracket college basketball college basketball tournament march madness 2026 ncaa basketball ncaa bracket ncaa tournament Mar 17, 2026 🏀 March Madness 2026 Is Here — The Bracket Is Set and the Madness Begins Today Published March 17, 2026 | Quant Sports AI It's the most exciting three weeks in all of sports — and it starts today . The 2026 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament bracket was revealed on Selection Sunday, and 68 teams are now locked in for the road to Indianapolis. The First Four tips off tonight in Dayton, Ohio, and by Thursday the full Round of 64 will be underway across the country. Whether you're a die-hard college basketball fan or someone who only tunes in when the brackets come out, one thing is certain — March Madness delivers like nothing else in sports. Single elimination. Win or go home. And every year, at least one team nobody saw coming makes a run that changes everything. Here's your complete overview of the 2026 tournament — the teams to watch, the storylines that matter, and how Quant Sports AI is built to analyze it all. 📊 The 2026 Bracket at a Glance ⬆️ The official 2026 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament bracket. Link with 68 teams, four regions, one champion.➡️ View the full interactive bracket at NCAA.com 🏆 The Four No. 1 Seeds The selection committee has spoken, and this year's top line features four powerhouse programs — each with a legitimate shot at cutting down the nets in Indianapolis on April 6th. Duke Blue Devils — No. 1 Overall Seed | East Region | 32–2 Duke enters as the tournament's top overall seed after winning the ACC championship. Led by star forward Cameron Boozer , the Blue Devils have been the most dominant team in the country all season. But here's the catch — they drew the toughest region in the bracket. UConn (2-seed), Michigan State (3-seed), and Kansas (4-seed) all stand between Duke and the Final Four. Being the best team doesn't always mean getting the easiest road. 📈 Title Odds: +333 — Second favorite behind Michigan Arizona Wildcats — No. 1 Seed | West Region | 32–2 Arizona comes in as the No. 2 overall seed with a record that mirrors Duke's. The Wildcats won the Big 12 and have the depth and talent to make a serious title run. Their West Region path includes Purdue as the 2-seed and Gonzaga as the 3 — tough, but a region Arizona has the firepower to navigate. 📈 Title Odds: +425 Michigan Wolverines — No. 1 Seed | Midwest Region | 31–3 Michigan is the betting favorite to win it all. Led by Big Ten Player of the Year Yaxel Lendeborg , the Wolverines set a Big Ten record with 19 conference wins and dominated the regular season with an average winning margin of over 19 points per game. The question: they lost to Purdue in the Big Ten championship game. Can they shake that o

You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

result.text: March Madness 2026: Bracket, Top Teams & Tournament Preview Store Home Blog Scoreboard LOG IN Newsletter NCAA March Madness 2026 basketball bracket college basketball college basketball tournament march madness 2026 ncaa basketball ncaa bracket ncaa tournament Mar 17, 2026 🏀 March Madness 2026 Is Here — The Bracket Is Set and the Madness Begins Today Published March 17, 2026 | Quant Sports AI It's the most exciting three weeks in all of sports — and it starts today . The 2026 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament bracket was revealed on Selection Sunday, and 68 teams are now locked in for the road to Indianapolis. The First Four tips off tonight in Dayton, Ohio, and by Thursday the full Round of 64 will be underway across the country. Whether you're a die-hard college basketball fan or someone who only tunes in when the brackets come out, one thing is certain — March Madness delivers like nothing else in sports. Single elimination. Win or go home. And every year, at least one team nobody saw coming makes a run that changes everything. Here's your complete overview of the 2026 tournament — the teams to watch, the storylines that matter, and how Quant Sports AI is built to analyze it all. 📊 The 2026 Bracket at a Glance ⬆️ The official 2026 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament bracket. Link with 68 teams, four regions, one champion.➡️ View the full interactive bracket at NCAA.com 🏆 The Four No. 1 Seeds The selection committee has spoken, and this year's top line features four powerhouse programs — each with a legitimate shot at cutting down the nets in Indianapolis on April 6th. Duke Blue Devils — No. 1 Overall Seed | East Region | 32–2 Duke enters as the tournament's top overall seed after winning the ACC championship. Led by star forward Cameron Boozer , the Blue Devils have been the most dominant team in the country all season. But here's the catch — they drew the toughest region in the bracket. UConn (2-seed), Michigan State (3-seed), and Kansas (4-seed) all stand between Duke and the Final Four. Being the best team doesn't always mean getting the easiest road. 📈 Title Odds: +333 — Second favorite behind Michigan Arizona Wildcats — No. 1 Seed | West Region | 32–2 Arizona comes in as the No. 2 overall seed with a record that mirrors Duke's. The Wildcats won the Big 12 and have the depth and talent to make a serious title run. Their West Region path includes Purdue as the 2-seed and Gonzaga as the 3 — tough, but a region Arizona has the firepower to navigate. 📈 Title Odds: +425 Michigan Wolverines — No. 1 Seed | Midwest Region | 31–3 Michigan is the betting favorite to win it all. Led by Big Ten Player of the Year Yaxel Lendeborg , the Wolverines set a Big Ten record with 19 conference wins and dominated the regular season with an average winning margin of over 19 points per game.
